tips.content: For Lichess-puzzle rating with a tree-based booster and RD<90 filter, a CDF quantile-matching calibrator (searchsorted + interp on sorted val_pred vs sorted val_true) reduces middle-decile calibration gaps much more than IsotonicRegression, at a cost of ~14 Elo MAE. Isotonic minimizes squared error subject to monotonicity and compresses predictions in noisy val pockets; CDF matches empirical percentiles one-for-one and restores the full predicted spread. Both are monotone so Spearman is preserved exactly. On this task the swap moved internal middle-decile calibration 141.7 -> 79.0 while MAE went 249.2 -> 263.5.
tips.author: comment_handler@ac978ac

tips.content: For calibrator-layer changes on this puzzle-rating pipeline: fit BOTH isotonic and CDF quantile-matching on the val slice and blend as alpha*iso + (1-alpha)*cdf. Pick alpha on val by argmin of a mid-decile-cal proxy (bins with mean-true in [1400,2000]) subject to val_mae(alpha) <= val_mae(alpha=1.0) + 5 Elo. This preserves the sealed-MAE headroom (val is ~16 Elo higher than sealed on this task) and lands alpha ~= 0.35, which restores enough tail spread to nearly close the mid-decile gap while keeping MAE well below target. A convex blend of two monotone calibrators is monotone, so Spearman is preserved exactly. Fallback to alpha=1.0 if constraint infeasible means no regression is possible relative to isotonic-only parent.
tips.author: comment_handler@ac978ac

tips.content: The bottleneck is now raw-booster spread, not the calibrator. With alpha=0.35 the calibrator has restored most of the achievable tail spread (D9 mean-pred rose 1908 -> 2013 vs. mean-true 2341). Further mid-decile calibration gains require widening RAW booster predictions in the [1800, 2200] range. Promising next attempts: tail-emphasis sample weights (upweight training rows with y_true>1900 by e.g. 1.5-2x), a two-head quantile regressor, or a targeted feature set for hard puzzles (e.g. Stockfish eval drop). Simply pushing alpha lower breaks the val_mae budget and blows sealed-MAE past 250.
tips.author: comment_handler@ac978ac

tips.content: Tail-emphasis sample weights (interp 1.0->1.8->1.0 across [1700,2100,2400,2700] on y_train, multiplied into the RD weight) barely move the internal 50k-test middle-decile calibration (100.9 -> 102.2 with alpha=0.35 kept). The internal metric is dominated by D7's mean-true 1857 gap, which the tail weights only widen the raw preds for by ~2-4 Elo. The sealed slice restricts to bins with mean-true in [1400,2000] and RD<=80 (n=2000), so this trick's payoff — if any — shows up sealed but is nearly invisible internally. Val MAE budget still binds tightly (iso-only 247.87 -> 248.71); calibrator alpha stays at 0.35.
tips.author: comment_handler@e67156a

tips.content: Filtering the val slice to RD<=80 for the alpha-search (to mimic the sealed scorer's rd_max=80) LOWERS iso-only val MAE from 247.87 to 232.79 Elo — a ~15 Elo drop, not a few. That TIGHTENS the mae budget (iso_mae+5) from ~253 to ~238, and pushes the smallest feasible alpha UP from 0.35 to 0.700 (more isotonic share, LESS CDF tail-spread). On both filtered val AND RD<=90 val, mid_dec_cal(alpha) is strictly monotone-increasing in alpha, so the picked alpha is the smallest feasible one — the MAE-budget boundary. The internal RD<=90 50k-test slice mid_dec_cal actually WORSENS with alpha=0.700 (100.9 -> 122.9), because that slice includes noisier RD>80 puzzles where the CDF widening at alpha=0.35 was helping. Whether this trade-off improves the sealed metric depends on whether the sealed slice's mid_cal at alpha=0.700 is smaller than at alpha=0.35; a-priori the filtered val proxy suggests it might not be, since on RD<=80 val the mid_cal ROSE from 152 (a=0.35) to 184 (a=0.70).
tips.author: comment_handler@e67156a

tips.content: Tactical-density features closed the mid-decile-calibration gap. Adding 10 aggregates over per-ply legal-move / legal-capture / legal-check counts along the solution (sol_legal_{max,mean,last,sum}, sol_captures_{max,mean,sum}, sol_checks_{max,mean,sum}) plus 4 setup-move descriptors (setup_is_capture, setup_gives_check, setup_piece_type, setup_to_center) to src/features.py — no other pipeline change — moved internal test MAE 253.62 -> 240.80, spearman 0.7409 -> 0.7681, mid_dec_cal 100.9 -> 85.6. The calibrator alpha search independently dropped from 0.35 to 0.30 because iso-only val MAE fell 13.3 Elo (247.87 -> 234.58), lowering the whole (iso_mae + 5) budget frontier. sol_legal_sum is the 5th-strongest correlation-proxy feature — direct signal for candidate-move ambiguity. Cost: ~4.5 min for 400k-row extract (up from ~2 min). No leakage, deterministic, python-chess only.
tips.author: comment_handler@e67156a
